seed: spark vs ash; hunger_vs_restraint; diagonal_argument; wave_vertical; radial against split_screen; jagged_edges; memory_vs_erasure; star; resilience_fragility | abstract variation request 76; target batch count 500
title: Rise vs Fall
reasoning: This composition translates the emotional tones of 'spark' and 'ash' into a dynamic interplay of bright and muted colors. The palette of black, dark violet, soft gold, and pale green embodies both hope and despair, where soft gold represents ignition and warmth (spark) while dark violet evokes the settled dust and fading warmth (ash). The composition flows diagonally, creating a distinct tension between the upward push of soft gold and the downward pull of dark violet, with jagged edges allowing for a sense of disruption throughout. The conflict between these energies is manifested in the collision of patterns: the veil of soft gold meets the crosshatch of dark violet, with structured chaos that interrupts monotony, representing the essence of denial versus acceptance. The arrangement incorporates broken pixels to introduce intentional imperfections and disruptions, enhancing the emotional depth of the work.

layout.name: pressure_corner
layout.description: One corner is overfilled and the rest of the square reacts with spreading marks.

composition.name: rise_vs_fall
composition.idea: Pixels move upward on one side and downward on the other.

referenceExample.title: Signal Inside Noise
referenceExample.pattern: one readable green path repeatedly interrupted by magenta static

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
